title: Risk heat maps

posts.author: Diego_Rodriguez (Diego A. Rodríguez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Monday.com Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like to submit a feature request that could greatly enhance the platform’s capabilities for risk management: a new type of visualization inspired by the bubble chart but tailored to create heat maps.

This feature would allow teams to manage risks more effectively by representing data points in a heat map format, where each bubble’s size and color would be dynamically adjusted based on the data.

Here are the key functionalities I envision for this feature:

1. Bubble size based on item count: The size of each bubble would be determined by the number of items in a specific quadrant. This would help users quickly identify areas with a high concentration of risks or tasks.
2. Gradual color scale (from red to green): The color of each bubble would follow a gradual color scale, from red (representing high risk) to green (representing low risk). This would allow for an intuitive, visual understanding of risk levels across the heat map.

I have created a dashboard with about 150 automations and 3 integrations with make.com that can use elements as risks and sub-elements as risk controls, so the heatmap would be the icing on the cake.
